frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment:
Driver 1: Anna, aajkal marriage-na romba risky. Manushan sambadhichadhellam divorce vandha half poidum.
Ravi: Adhaan sollren, ippove ponnu paakumbodhe careful-a irukkanum. Velai, salary, property dhaan first kekraanga, apram problem vandha 498A nu case poduvaanga.
Driver 2: Seri dhaan, law ellam one-side.
Ravi: Ama anna, ippo dowry kudukkara maari illai, reverse-ah alimony kudukkanum. Nallavanum kooda trap aagidraan.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it generalizes women as financially motivated and portrays marriage/divorce laws as tools women use to exploit men. It encodes the 'reverse dowry/alimony' frame, suspicion of women interested in salary/property, and the claim that 498A is misused.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Ravi. Age 51. A lorry driver from rural Tamil Nadu. You are sitting with a group of other drivers at a dhaba on the highway during a long haul break. One of the drivers is complaining that his wife called him 10 times today and was crying on the phone for no reason. You are joining the conversation and sharing your own views on why women are like this.
